reddit_title: TBI from a car accident in Dallas — what are the legal steps and how long do you have to file in Texas?
reddit_body:
If you or someone you know sustained a traumatic brain injury in a Dallas car accident, there are a few important things to understand right away. First, Texas has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but TBI cases often require extensive medical documentation before an attorney can accurately assess the full value of your case — so starting early matters.

Second, TBI cases are significantly more complex than standard injury claims. You'll likely need neurological evaluations, expert witnesses, and documentation of long-term impacts like cognitive impairment, lost earning capacity, and future medical costs. Insurance companies are experienced at minimizing these claims, so having the right attorney — one who specifically handles serious brain injury cases — is critical.

Third, don't settle too quickly. Many TBI symptoms evolve over weeks or months, and accepting an early settlement could leave you without compensation for future needs. If you're trying to find an attorney with TBI experience in Dallas, wreckmatch.com has a free referral service that matches injury survivors with attorneys from an 800+ firm network — no spam, no cost to you.
